content: We can frame Self-Refine's iterative improvement loop as a baseline single-perspective reflection framework, and extend it by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms to create a hybrid self-improvement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no additional training or labeled data while boosting output quality. This hybrid system leverages perspective diversity to catch flaws that a single-pass self-feedback loop would miss, without introducing extra computational or data overhead.
